phenomenon: cultural-identity threat shapes political institutional trust
claim: Individuals who perceive newcomers as threatening shared national identity show reduced confidence in political institutions and elites.

phenomenon: cross-domain cognitive transfer from language switching
claim: Bilingual language switching experience enhances general cognitive flexibility in non-linguistic task switching by improving stimulus-response reconfiguration.

phenomenon: representativeness heuristic applied retrospectively to sequence inference
claim: Rare outcomes are inferred to come from longer sequences because people expect small samples not to contain rare events.

phenomenon: regret priming as debiasing mechanism for preference reversals
claim: Making anticipated regret salient induces critical self-examination that eliminates preference for superficially justified but logically weak options.
Alternative causal explanations displace false causal inferences more effectively than denials or counter-evidence because they prevent the continued influence effect.

phenomenon: situational stereotype activation reduces threatened group performance
claim: Merely activating gender stereotypes during cognitive tests reduces female performance and enhances male performance through mechanisms of anxiety and cognitive interference.
